channel.title: LAMG's personal blog
channel.description: Recent content on LAMG's personal blog

item.title: Using git over SSH in Cuba or drink your favorite poison
item.description: Using Git over the HTTPS protocol has the shortcoming, at least on my usual Linux setup interacting with GitHub, of asking user name and password for every modification on the remote branch. The solution for that is using git over SSH. You upload your SSH public key to GitHub, add the remote branch with an URL like git@github.com:user/repository.git, and after that the communication is smoother. I have the suspicion that is even more secure.

item.title: Using Go in Cuba
item.description: Some packages and tools related to Go programming cannot be downloaded from Cuba, due some restrictions Google applies. The savior for such cases turns out to be Tor Browser, with a little addition. These restrictions hit your face when you do something like: git clone git@github.com:lamg/proxy.git cd proxy/cmd/proxy go install since that project has dependencies hosted at the official Go site, blocked from Cuba, which are golang.org/x/net and golang.org/x/text. In this case, it is easy to download them manually from https://github.

item.title: About
item.description: Programmer A step closer to God when objects outside our mind behave in a way predicted by symbol manipulation. Pursuing this kind of stuff: (x, y := x + 1, y + 1).(x - y) = {substitution} x + 1 - (y - 1) = {arithmetic} x - y Technical skills Programming languages: Go, Haskell, Python, C#, C, Lisp Operating systems: Linux (Debian, Ubuntu), Plan 9, Windows Networking: HTTP, TCP/IP Professional experience Internship 02/2017 - 07/2017 at Universidad de Pinar del Río Update MS Active Directory with data from a PostgreSQL database adsync Programmer 09/2017 - 11/2019 at Universidad de Pinar del Río HTTP proxy server with download quotas per user in a period of time Other projects available at my GitHub profile Backend developer 12/2019 - present Talon.
